confirmed_bridges.query_title: On Associativity Equations in Dispersionless Integrable Hierarchies
confirmed_bridges.cand_title: Three constructions of Frobenius manifolds: a comparative study
confirmed_bridges.shared_mechanism: The WDVV/associativity equations that paper A solves are exactly the defining equations of the Frobenius manifolds that paper B constructs and compares: a potential F whose third derivatives yield associative structure constants in flat coordinates. Both papers are about the same governing object (a solution of WDVV = a Frobenius-manifold structure), reached from integrable-hierarchy tau-functions in A and from quantum cohomology / singularity theory / mirror-symmetry constructions in B, so the underlying mathematical machinery is shared.

mapping.a_element: prepotential F whose third derivatives give structure constants satisfying the WDVV associativity equations
mapping.b_element: Frobenius manifold potential whose third derivatives define the associative Frobenius-algebra multiplication on tangent spaces

mapping.a_element: the associativity (WDVV) equations themselves
mapping.b_element: the associativity condition that defines a Frobenius manifold structure

mapping.a_element: the infinitely-many (or finitely-many) flat times / variables of the dispersionless hierarchy on which F depends
mapping.b_element: the flat coordinates on the Frobenius manifold

mapping.a_element: tau-function of dispersionless KP/Toda hierarchy furnishing a solution to WDVV
mapping.b_element: geometric constructions (quantum cohomology, Saito unfolding of singularities, Barannikov-Kontsevich) furnishing a Frobenius manifold

confirmed_bridges.query_title: Updating Probabilities with Data and Moments
confirmed_bridges.cand_title: Nonthermal steady states after an interaction quench in the Falicov-Kimball model
confirmed_bridges.shared_mechanism: Paper A derives the canonical exponential-family distribution obtained by maximizing (relative) entropy subject to moment constraints, and Paper B describes its post-quench steady state via a generalized Gibbs ensemble — which is exactly that same maximum-entropy-under-constraints construction, with the conserved-quantity expectation values playing the role of A's moments and the GGE's Lagrange multipliers playing the role of A's; the shared machinery is the MaxEnt/Gibbs variational principle. The rest of B's dynamical-mean-field-theory time evolution is not shared, but the statistical characterization of its endpoint maps role-for-role onto A's method.

mapping.a_element: maximizing (relative) entropy subject to a set of constraints to fix the distribution
mapping.b_element: constructing the generalized Gibbs ensemble by maximizing entropy subject to the conserved-quantity constraints

mapping.a_element: the imposed moment constraints ⟨f_i⟩ = F_i on the distribution
mapping.b_element: the fixed expectation values of the model's conserved quantities (integrals of motion) that define the GGE

mapping.a_element: Lagrange multipliers λ_i conjugate to the moment constraints
mapping.b_element: the generalized inverse temperatures / Lagrange multipliers of the GGE conjugate to each conserved quantity

mapping.a_element: the resulting canonical (exponential-family) posterior distribution
mapping.b_element: the GGE steady-state density matrix ρ ∝ exp(−Σ λ_i I_i)

confirmed_bridges.query_title: Casimir forces in a T operator approach
confirmed_bridges.cand_title: Periodic Orbit Theory and Spectral Statistics for Quantum Graphs
confirmed_bridges.shared_mechanism: Both are the same scattering-determinant machinery: a spectral quantity is written as Tr ln(1 - [scattering operator]) — a T-operator round-trip T_a G0 T_b G0 for the Casimir energy (A) versus the bond S-matrix det(1 - S(k)) for the graph spectrum (B) — and the log-det is expanded as a geometric series whose n-th term sums over closed multiple-scattering paths (A) = periodic orbits (B). The T-operators map role-for-role onto the bond scattering amplitudes and the free Green's function onto the bond propagation phase, so the trace-formula / multiple-scattering expansion is the shared underlying method.

mapping.a_element: transition (T) operator describing scattering off each body
mapping.b_element: per-bond/vertex scattering amplitude (unitary S-matrix element) of the quantized graph

mapping.a_element: free Green's function G0 propagating the field between scatterers
mapping.b_element: free propagation phase exp(ikL) along a bond of length L

mapping.a_element: round-trip operator T_a G0 T_b G0 built from T-operators and propagators
mapping.b_element: bond-scattering evolution operator S(k) that maps directed bonds to directed bonds

mapping.a_element: Casimir energy written as Tr ln(1 - T_a G0 T_b G0) (Kenneth-Klich scattering formula)
mapping.b_element: secular determinant det(1 - S(k)) whose ln-det/trace generates the quantum spectrum

mapping.a_element: multiple-scattering expansion: Tr ln(1-.) as a sum over closed paths bouncing between the bodies
mapping.b_element: periodic-orbit trace formula: the same geometric expansion as a sum over periodic orbits on the graph

mapping.a_element: Casimir (zero-point) energy / force being computed
mapping.b_element: density of states / quantum spectrum being computed

confirmed_bridges.query_title: Cluster abundances and S-Z power spectra: effects of non-Gaussianity and early dark energy
confirmed_bridges.cand_title: Mesoscopic gap fluctuations in an unconventional superconductor
confirmed_bridges.shared_mechanism: Both derive the abundance and spatial arrangement of discrete objects from the tail of a fluctuating random field's probability distribution above a critical threshold: paper A computes cluster counts from the density-field PDF (Gaussian/chi^2) exceeding the collapse threshold, and paper B computes superconducting islands from the derived PDF of local pairing temperatures exceeding the local ordering condition above the mean-field transition. The collapse threshold plays the role of the local pairing condition, and enhancing the PDF tail (non-Gaussianity / disorder fluctuations) increases the number of discrete objects in both, so the threshold-crossing/rare-region statistics is the same machinery.

mapping.a_element: primordial density fluctuation field
mapping.b_element: disorder-induced local pairing-temperature field

mapping.a_element: probability distribution of the density field (Gaussian vs chi^2_1 non-Gaussian)
mapping.b_element: derived probability distribution function of local pairing temperatures/gaps

mapping.a_element: critical collapse threshold above which a region forms a cluster
mapping.b_element: local condition for pairing above the mean-field transition (region becomes a superconducting island)

mapping.a_element: collapsed galaxy clusters
mapping.b_element: superconducting islands (embedded in metallic regions)

mapping.a_element: cluster abundance / number counts set by the high-density tail of the PDF
mapping.b_element: abundance/network of superconducting islands set by the tail of the pairing-temperature PDF

mapping.a_element: non-Gaussianity enhancing the tail to raise cluster abundance
mapping.b_element: mesoscopic disorder fluctuations enhancing the tail to produce islands above the mean-field T_c

confirmed_bridges.query_title: Phase diagram and complexity of mode-locked lasers: from order to disorder
confirmed_bridges.shared_mechanism: Both rest on the same spin-glass machinery: quenched disorder plus frustration/competition producing a rugged landscape with an exponentially large number of frozen/metastable states and hyper-sensitivity to perturbations. Paper A computes this explicitly (mean-field Hamiltonian, phase diagram, spin-glass phase of frozen field phases) for mode-locked lasers, while paper B advocates the identical spin-glass framework — competition + heterogeneity generating metastable stasis punctuated by crises and hyper-sensitivity — for economic systems, so a coherent role-for-role mapping holds.

mapping.a_element: phases of the electromagnetic modes acting as Ising-like spins
mapping.b_element: heterogeneous economic agents as the interacting degrees of freedom

mapping.a_element: structural randomness / multiple-scattering disorder giving quenched random couplings
mapping.b_element: heterogeneities among agents (quenched disorder)

mapping.a_element: nonlinear mode competition in the gain medium
mapping.b_element: competition among agents

mapping.a_element: spin-glass phase: field phases frozen in an exponentially large number of configurations
mapping.b_element: long epochs of stasis arising from many metastable states

mapping.a_element: hyper-sensitivity / criticality near the spin-glass transition
mapping.b_element: hyper-sensitivity to small changes of the environment and crises

mapping.a_element: mean-field spin-glass Hamiltonian and its phase diagram
mapping.b_element: spin-glass models and agent-based simulations proposed for the economy

confirmed_bridges.cand_title: Phase diagram and complexity of mode-locked lasers: from order to disorder
confirmed_bridges.shared_mechanism: Both rest on the same spin-glass machinery: frustration from competition/heterogeneity (A) or a random mean-field Hamiltonian (B) produces a rugged landscape with an exponentially large number of frozen metastable states, giving stasis punctuated by transitions and hypersensitivity. A invokes spin-glass theory as the mechanism behind endogenous market complexity, while B computes it explicitly as a phase diagram for mode-locked lasers, so the underlying method corresponds role-for-role.

mapping.a_element: competition among economic agents combined with heterogeneities
mapping.b_element: quenched randomness of the mean-field Hamiltonian set by the degree of structural disorder

mapping.a_element: long epochs of stasis (the economy stuck in a metastable state)
mapping.b_element: spin-glass phase in which the field phases are frozen in an exponentially large number of configurations

mapping.a_element: crises interrupting the calm (abrupt regime changes)
mapping.b_element: transitions between the paramagnetic/ferromagnetic/spin-glass phases in the pumping-rate vs disorder phase diagram

mapping.a_element: hyper-sensitivity to small changes of the environment
mapping.b_element: the rugged energy landscape of the spin-glass phase with its many nearly-degenerate metastable states

mapping.a_element: spin-glass framing of the complexity of the economic system
mapping.b_element: spin-glass mean-field Hamiltonian describing the mode-locked laser modes

confirmed_bridges.query_title: Quasispecies Theory for Horizontal Gene Transfer and Recombination
confirmed_bridges.cand_title: Simple Glass Models and their Quantum Annealing
confirmed_bridges.shared_mechanism: Both reduce to finding the extremal eigenstate of a mean-field/permutation-invariant spin Hamiltonian written as a diagonal landscape term (fitness in A, random energy in B) plus an off-diagonal field (the mutation operator in A plays exactly the role of the transverse field in B), obtained via a variational/maximum principle. Each shows a sharp transition at which the extremal eigenstate localizes onto a single peak/ground state (A's error threshold onto the sharp fitness peak, B's first-order quantum transition projecting onto the unperturbed ground state), with the sharp-peak landscape corresponding to the (quantum) REM.

mapping.a_element: steady-state mean fitness (largest eigenvalue of the mutation-selection evolution operator)
mapping.b_element: ground-state energy (lowest eigenvalue of the quantum spin-glass Hamiltonian)

mapping.a_element: point-mutation term, off-diagonal in genotype space (Crow-Kimura/Eigen)
mapping.b_element: transverse field, the off-diagonal spin-flip term

mapping.a_element: fitness / replication-rate landscape, diagonal over genotypes
mapping.b_element: random energy landscape, diagonal over spin configurations

mapping.a_element: single sharp peak fitness landscape
mapping.b_element: (quantum) Random Energy Model

mapping.a_element: maximum principle / variational solution for the mean fitness
mapping.b_element: mean-field solution for the ground state

mapping.a_element: selected/localized phase (population condensed on the fittest genotype)
mapping.b_element: phase where the eigenstate projects onto the unperturbed ground state

mapping.a_element: selection phase transition / error threshold
mapping.b_element: first-order quantum phase transition

confirmed_bridges.query_title: Superharmonic Perturbations of a Gaussian Measure, Equilibrium Measures and Orthogonal Polynomials
confirmed_bridges.cand_title: Semiclassical Soliton Ensembles for the Focusing Nonlinear Schroedinger Equation
confirmed_bridges.shared_mechanism: Both run the same machinery: a 2x2 matrix Riemann-Hilbert/d-bar problem analyzed in a semiclassical limit, where a scalar complex phase function is fixed by a potential-theoretic variational/equilibrium principle and the geometry of the support controls the asymptotics. Paper A's weighted-energy equilibrium measure plays the role of paper B's Lax-Levermore-type variational principle, and its Schwarz-function curve corresponds to B's finite-gap band structure.

mapping.a_element: 2x2 matrix d-bar problem encoding the (planar) orthogonal polynomials
mapping.b_element: 2x2 matrix Riemann-Hilbert problem encoding the NLS solutions (d-bar generalizes RH)

mapping.a_element: weighted-energy equilibrium measure / variational problem in the external field U^mu
mapping.b_element: variational principle characterizing the phase via critical points of a functional, generalizing the Lax-Levermore principle

mapping.a_element: semi-classical scaling limit of the orthogonal polynomials
mapping.b_element: semiclassical limit of the focusing nonlinear Schroedinger equation

mapping.a_element: Schwarz function of the curve bounding the support of the equilibrium measure
mapping.b_element: finite-gap ansatz fixing the scalar complex phase function and its band/gap geometry

mapping.a_element: asymptotic distribution of the zeros of the orthogonal polynomials
mapping.b_element: strong locally uniform asymptotics of the exact NLS solutions in regions of the (x,t) plane
